# Evidence — Leg W7: proactive suggestions completion (#8792) + GLSL shader e2e tail (#10694)

Branch `feat/ui-mobile-gap-burndown` @ develop `5471346e7a6`. Captured 2026-07-02.

## Research findings inventory (verified against THIS tip before coding)

### #8792 reopen items — state on tip

| Reopen item | State on tip | Evidence |
| --- | --- | --- |
| (a) Settings exposure of off/subtle/chatty | **ALREADY SHIPPED on tip** — `packages/ui/src/components/settings/CapabilitiesSection.tsx` renders the `capability-proactive-suggestions` segmented control (Off / Subtle / Chatty) with the hint "How often the agent offers a helpful suggestion when you switch views or run a shortcut", persisting `ELIZA_PROACTIVE_INTERACTIONS` via `client.updateConfig`; registered through the existing settings-sections wiring. The reopen text ("env-only today") is stale for this item — no new settings section was added by this leg (adding a duplicate control would violate single-source; noted for epic-B settings consolidation coordination). Verified by grep AND by rendered pixels: this leg built the Storybook catalog fresh from this tree and screenshotted the real story. | `settings-screenshots/capabilities-section-full.png`, `capabilities-proactive-control.png`, `capabilities-proactive-options-open.png` (captured by `capture-storybook.mjs` in that dir, port 36700) |
| (b) accept/'Do it' affordance | **ALREADY SHIPPED on tip** — `packages/ui/src/components/composites/chat/chat-message.tsx` renders the `Suggestion` chip + **Do it** (`onAcceptSuggestion`) + dismiss (`onDismissSuggestion`) for assistant messages with `source === "proactive-interaction"`; wired in `ChatView.tsx` (`handleAcceptSuggestion` → `sendChatText("Yes, let's do it.")` + local dismiss). This leg does not re-implement it — it puts the affordance under a REAL e2e for the first time (see (c)); the e2e screenshots double as rendered proof. | `suggestions-e2e/sg-01…sg-04.png` + `walkthrough.webm` |
| (c) real e2e (real event path, real governor) | **WAS THE GAP — CLOSED by this leg.** Existing coverage stopped at the WS frame (`packages/agent/src/api/proactive-interaction-pipeline.test.ts`) with a vitest-mocked clock; nothing rendered a suggestion or clicked it. **NEW:** `packages/ui/src/components/pages/__e2e__/run-suggestions-e2e.mjs` (+ `suggestions-fixture.tsx`), `bun run --cwd packages/ui test:suggestions-e2e`, CI-wired in `.github/workflows/ui-e2e-gate.yml`. Phase 1 drives the REAL server modules (real `POST /api/views/:id/navigate` + `POST /api/interactions/shortcut` handlers + real `SLASH_COMMAND_INVOKED` event → the real registered decider with its real debounce timers → the REAL `ProactiveInteractionGate` (an observing subclass records `super.tryAdmit`'s verdicts — the governor itself is untouched) → the real `routeAutonomyTextToUser` (memory persist + broadcast)). The 9-step ledger proves: admit; burst suppressed (**global cooldown**); slash policy-silent (judge never called); control-shortcut denied pre-judge; agent-initiated skip; clock-advanced admit; **per-surface cooldown**; `off` kill-switch pre-judge; **textual dedup**. Phase 2 feeds the two REAL captured frames through the real `parseProactiveMessageEvent` parser into the real `ChatTranscript`/`ChatMessage` composites in headless Chromium and clicks the real buttons: dismiss removes the bubble (nothing sent), **Do it** sends `"Yes, let's do it."` and clears the bubble; malformed / foreign-conversation / replayed frames are rejected/deduped. The judge model output is scripted in the runner (the judge is not the subject; the LIVE judge trajectory is separate evidence, below). | `suggestions-e2e/` (`run-log.txt` incl. gate ledger, `phase1-pipeline.json` with the raw frames + gate decisions, `sg-*.png`, `walkthrough.webm`) |
| (d) recordings of rate-limit + dismiss | **NEW** — the suggestions e2e records the whole flow (suggestion appears → burst suppressed → dismiss → accept) as `walkthrough.webm` + per-step screenshots; the suppression evidence is the gate ledger in `run-log.txt`/`phase1-pipeline.json` (reasons: `global cooldown`, `per-surface cooldown`, `duplicate of a recent comment`). `audit:app` cannot exercise this flow (a suggestion only exists after a server-pushed governed frame; the audit walks static views) — see N/A note. | `suggestions-e2e/walkthrough.webm`, `run-log.txt` |

### #10694 tail — state on tip

- `run-background-e2e.mjs` launched Chromium with `--disable-gpu` → WebGL unavailable → `ProgrammableShaderBackground` always took the `no-webgl` fallback, so the GLSL path was **never rendered** in the e2e. **FIXED:** the runner now launches SwiftShader ANGLE (`--use-gl=angle --use-angle=swiftshader --enable-unsafe-swiftshader`), applies a real GLSL preset through the REAL `background:apply` channel, asserts the `app-background-glsl` canvas stays live (no fallback swap), and pixel-probes the COMPOSITED frames (screenshot → decode → 96-sample grid): pattern variance (spread=133, unique=70 on the recorded run), frame-over-frame animation delta (u_time; delta≈15), u_color recolor response (green channel dominant), unknown-preset-id ignored, and undo integration back through GLSL configs to the prior color field. CI-wired in `ui-e2e-gate.yml` (previously this runner ran in NO workflow).
- Live-LLM BACKGROUND scenario: **NEW** `packages/scenario-runner/test/scenarios/live-background-actions.scenario.ts` (lane `live-only`) — REAL Cerebras `gpt-oss-120b` routes natural chat ("make the app background teal" / "switch to the animated lava shader" / "undo that") to the real plugin-app-control BACKGROUND handler; the final checks assert the real `background:apply` broadcast payloads (2× set incl. one `mode:"glsl"`, 1× undo). Report + trajectories hand-reviewed (see `live-llm/REVIEW.md`).
- **Genuine live-routing finding kept as evidence:** with the first, oblique phrasing ("give me one of the animated shader backgrounds — something like a lava lamp", no preset named) the live planner routed to REPLY ("I'm not sure how to answer that") instead of BACKGROUND — `live-llm/attempt1-oblique-phrasing-report.json` + `attempt1-run-log.txt`. The committed scenario names the advertised preset so the lane is a stable regression; the oblique-phrasing miss is a real model-routing gap worth a note on #10694.
- The BACKGROUND op-enum alignment is owned elsewhere and untouched here, per wave coordination.

## Artifacts

- `background-e2e/` — SwiftShader GLSL run: `run-log.txt` (pixel-probe numbers), `bg-01…bg-12.png` (09–12 are the GLSL steps), `walkthrough.webm`. Hand-reviewed: bg-09 shows the rasterized teal plasma (bright hotspot, smooth field), bg-11 the emerald recolor of the SAME live shader.
- `suggestions-e2e/` — `run-log.txt` (31 assertions + gate ledger), `phase1-pipeline.json` (raw captured frames, gate decisions, judge-call count, memory count), `sg-01…sg-04.png`, `walkthrough.webm`.
- `live-llm/` — `live-background-actions-report.json` (PASSED; 3 BACKGROUND calls success incl. `{"op":"set","preset":"lava"}` → `values {op:set, mode:glsl, presetId:lava}`), `run/` (run viewer + trajectories), `live-background-actions-native.jsonl` (+ manifest; 25 rows, real Cerebras tool-calls visible), `run-log.txt`, `attempt1-*` (the preserved oblique-phrasing failure), `live-judge-wallet-sample.json` (REAL #8792 judge prompt → live Cerebras output → real parser), `REVIEW.md` (hand-review notes).
- `settings-screenshots/` — fresh Storybook build of this tree → real `Settings/CapabilitiesSection` story: full section, the Proactive suggestions control, and the Off/Subtle/Chatty options (`capture-storybook.mjs` reproduces it).

## N/A rows

- **audit:app full loop** — N/A for the rate-limit + dismiss recording specifically: the audit harness walks views without a live agent pushing governed `proactive-message` frames, so the suggestion bubble never exists in its captures. The suggestions-e2e video records exactly that behavior against the real pipeline instead, and is CI-gated.
- **Physical-device capture** — N/A: no native/mobile surface changed in this leg (browser-rendered UI + server pipeline + e2e runners + a live-only scenario).
- **Audio/narrated walkthrough** — N/A: no voice/TTS/STT surface touched.
